mission.title: Install and Configure Metal3 Bare Metal Operator on Kubernetes
mission.description: Metal3 (Metal Kubed) provides a Kubernetes-native API for managing bare metal infrastructure. The Bare Metal Operator maintains an inventory of available hosts as BareMetalHost custom resources and can inspect hardware, provision hosts with images, and clean disk contents.

steps.title: Install cert-manager (prerequisite)
steps.description:
The Bare Metal Operator requires cert-manager for webhook TLS certificates.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/releases/download/v1.16.3/cert-manager.yaml
kubectl wait --for=condition=Available --timeout=300s deployment/cert-manager -n cert-manager
```

steps.title: Deploy the Bare Metal Operator using kustomize
steps.description:
Apply the rendered manifests from the official repository to deploy the Bare Metal Operator.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metal3-io/baremetal-operator/main/config/render/capm3.yaml
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Bare Metal Operator pods are running and the BareMetalHost CRD is registered.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n baremetal-operator-system
kubectl get crd baremetalhosts.metal3.io
```

steps.title: Create a sample BareMetalHost resource
steps.description:
Register a bare metal host with the operator by creating a BareMetalHost resource.
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: metal3.io/v1alpha1
kind: BareMetalHost
metadata:
  name: example-host
  namespace: baremetal-operator-system
spec:
  online: true
  bmc:
    address: ipmi://192.168.1.100
    credentialsName: example-host-bmc-secret
  bootMACAddress: "00:11:22:33:44:55"
EOF
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Bare Metal Operator running in the baremetal-operator-system namespace with the BareMetalHost CRD available. You can then register and manage bare metal hosts through the Kubernetes API.

uninstall.title: Remove BareMetalHost resources
uninstall.description: Delete all BareMetalHost resources before removing the operator.

uninstall.title: Remove the operator
uninstall.description: Delete the operator manifests and CRDs.

upgrade.title: Backup BareMetalHost resources
upgrade.description: Export all BareMetalHost resources before upgrading the operator.

upgrade.title: Apply latest manifests
upgrade.description: Re-apply the latest rendered manifests from the repository.

troubleshooting.title: Operator pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure cert-manager is running and webhook certificates are generated.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager
kubectl logs -n baremetal-operator-system -l control-plane=controller-manager
```

troubleshooting.title: BareMetalHost stuck in registering state
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the BMC credentials secret exists and the BMC address is reachable from the cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe baremetalhost example-host -n baremetal-operator-system
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher with cert-manager installed. Bare metal hosts with IPMI/Redfish BMC access are needed for full functionality.
